revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前まで...'
revision.text:
'''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは'''物部神社'''または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。 [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|300px|拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）]]
==概要== 旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧物部郷の[[総社]]。祭神は、主祭神の物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命と、相殿神の住吉神・猿田彦神を合わせて祀る。 [[649年]]に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。
==境内== [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] 三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は、[[1497年]]に[[佐々木高頼]]の寄進により再建されたもので、[[1913年]]に国の特別保護建造物に、戦後は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立された。
==文化財== *本殿 - 1497年築、前室付三間社流造、檜皮葺。国の重要文化財。 *松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。県選択無形民族文化財。
==建築概要== *所在地 - 〒 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8
==交通アクセス== *[[JR琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅]] 徒歩10分
{{Commons|Category:Katsube-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}}
[[Category:守山市]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]]

'''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは'''物部神社'''または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。 [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|300px|拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）]]
==概要== 旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧物部郷の[[総社]]。祭神は、主祭神の物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命と、相殿神の住吉神・猿田彦神を合わせて祀る。 [[649年]]に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。
==境内== [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] 三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は、[[1497年]]に[[佐々木高頼]]の寄進により再建されたもので、[[1913年]]に国の特別保護建造物に、戦後は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立された。
==文化財== *本殿 - 1497年築、前室付三間社流造、檜皮葺。国の重要文化財。 *松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。県選択無形民族文化財。
==所在地== 〒524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8
==交通アクセス== *[[JR琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅]] 徒歩10分

'''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは'''物部神社'''または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。 [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|300px|拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）]]
==概要== 旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧物部郷の[[総社]]。祭神は、主祭神の物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命と、相殿神の住吉神・猿田彦神を合わせて祀る。 [[649年]]に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。
==境内== [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有する。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は、[[1497年]]に[[佐々木高頼]]の寄進により再建されたもので、[[1913年]]に国の特別保護建造物に、戦後は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立された。
==文化財== *本殿 - 1497年築、前室付三間社流造、檜皮葺。国の重要文化財。 *松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。県選択無形民族文化財。
==所在地== 〒524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8
==交通アクセス== *[[JR琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅]] 徒歩10分

'''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは'''物部神社'''または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。 [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|300px|拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）]]
==概要== 旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧物部郷の[[総社]]。祭神は、主祭神の物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命と、相殿神の住吉神・猿田彦神を合わせて祀る。 [[649年]]に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。江戸時代初期に当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。

==文化財== *本殿 - 1497年築、前室付三間社流造、檜皮葺。国の重要文化財。 *松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。県選択無形民族文化財。
==所在地== 〒524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8

{{出典の明記|date=2013年12月27日 (金) 13:21 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称 = 勝部神社| 所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]1-8-8| 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=勝部神社}}| 祭神 = 物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命| 創建 = 大化5年（649年）| 社格 = 郷社| 例祭 =| 神事 = 1月第2土曜日（松明祭）| 本殿 = | 画像= [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）}} [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] '''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは物部神社または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。 == 概要 == 旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧[[物部郷]]の[[総社]]。祭神は、主祭神の物部布津命・火明命・宇麻志間知命と、相殿神の住吉神・猿田彦神を合わせて祀る。[[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。江戸時代初期に当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。 == 境内 == 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有する。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は、[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼|佐々木高頼]]の寄進により再建されたもので、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に国の特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定されている。顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立された。 == 文化財 == * 本殿 - 1497年（明応6年）建立、三間社流造、檜皮葺。国の重要文化財。 * 松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。毎年1月第2土曜日に行われる。県選択無形民俗文化財。 == 所在地 == [[〒]]524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅 (滋賀県)|守山駅]] 徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部氏|社かつへ]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]]

{{出典の明記|date=2013年12月27日 (金) 13:21 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称 = 勝部神社| 所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]1-8-8| 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=勝部神社}}| 祭神 = 物部布津神（[[天火明命]]・[[ウマシマジ|宇麻志間知命]]・[[経津主神|布津主神]]）| 創建 = [[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）| 社格 = 郷社| 例祭 =| 神事 = 1月第2土曜日（松明祭）| 本殿 = | 画像= [[画像:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）}} [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] '''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは物部神社または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧[[物部郷]]の[[総社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）8月13日に物部宿禰広国が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月には正六位上を、[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]）10月には従五位下の[[神位]]を授かる。 [[室町時代]]には[[近江国|近江]][[守護]][[六角氏]]により篤い信仰を受けており、六角軍が出陣する際にはその旗竿は決まって当社の竹林の竹を使用するほどであった。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼]]の寄進により再建されたものである。 [[元亀]]年間（[[1570年]]～[[1573年]]）には[[織田信長]]が[[野洲郡]]・[[栗太郡]]の起請文60通を当社に納めている。[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）8月には[[豊臣秀次]]によって神田の寄進と本殿の修理が行われている。 [[江戸時代]]には当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）に国の特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定されている。 江戸時代まで当社は物部神社または物部大明神と呼ばれてきたが、[[明治]]時代の初めに勝部神社に改めている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、郷社となる。 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有している。 顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立されたものである。 == 祭神 == *主祭神－物部布津神（[[天火明命]]・[[ウマシマジ|宇麻志間知命]]・[[経津主神|布津主神]]） *合祀神－[[住吉三神|住吉神]]・[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] == 境内 == *本殿 *拝殿 *産土社 *顕彰殿 *祓所 *鐘楼 == 文化財 == * 本殿 - [[重要文化財]]。[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）建立。三間社流造、檜皮葺。 * 松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。毎年1月第2土曜日に行われる。県選択[[無形民俗文化財]]。 == 所在地 == [[〒]]524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅 (滋賀県)|守山駅]] 徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部氏|社かつへ]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]]

拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥）}} [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] '''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは物部神社または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧[[物部郷]]の[[総社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）8月13日に物部宿禰広国（もののべのすくねひろくに）が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月には正六位上を、[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]）10月には従五位下の[[神位]]を授かる。 [[室町時代]]には[[近江国|近江]][[守護]][[六角氏]]により篤い信仰を受けており、六角軍が出陣する際にはその旗竿は決まって当社の竹林の竹を使用するほどであった。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼]]の寄進により再建されたものである。 [[元亀]]年間（[[1570年]] - [[1573年]]）には[[織田信長]]が[[野洲郡]]・[[栗太郡]]の起請文60通を当社に納めている。[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）8月には[[豊臣秀次]]によって神田の寄進と本殿の修理が行われている。 [[江戸時代]]には当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）に国の特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定されている。 江戸時代まで当社は物部神社または物部大明神と呼ばれてきたが、[[明治]]時代の初めに勝部神社に改めている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、郷社となる。 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有している。 顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立されたものである。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - 物部布津神（[[天火明命]]・[[ウマシマジ|宇麻志間知命]]・[[経津主神|布津主神]]） *合祀神 - [[住吉三神|住吉神]]・[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] == 境内 == *本殿 *拝殿 *産土社 *顕彰殿 *祓所 *鐘楼 == 文化財 == ===重要文化財=== * 本殿 - [[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）建立。三間社流造、檜皮葺。 ===県選択無形民俗文化財=== * 松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。毎年1月第2土曜日に行われる。 == 所在地 == [[〒]]524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅 (滋賀県)|守山駅]] 徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部氏|社かつへ]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]]

拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥） }} [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] '''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[守山市]][[勝部 (守山市)|勝部]]にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は'''物部郷総社 勝部神社'''。戦前までは物部神社または'''物部大明神'''、'''勝部大明神'''と称されていた。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧[[物部郷]]の[[総社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）8月13日に物部宿禰広国（もののべのすくねひろくに）が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月には正六位上を、[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]）10月には従五位下の[[神位]]を授かる。 [[室町時代]]には[[近江国|近江]][[守護]][[六角氏]]により篤い信仰を受けており、六角軍が出陣する際にはその旗竿は決まって当社の竹林の竹を使用するほどであった。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼]]の寄進により再建されたものである。 [[元亀]]年間（[[1570年]] - [[1573年]]）には[[織田信長]]が[[野洲郡]]・[[栗太郡]]の起請文60通を当社に納めている。[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）8月には[[豊臣秀次]]によって神田の寄進と本殿の修理が行われている。 [[江戸時代]]には当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）に国の特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定されている。 江戸時代まで当社は物部神社または物部大明神と呼ばれてきたが、[[明治]]時代の初めに勝部神社に改めている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、郷社となる。 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有している。 顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立されたものである。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - 物部布津神（[[天火明命]]・[[ウマシマジ|宇麻志間知命]]・[[経津主神|布津主神]]） *合祀神 - [[住吉三神|住吉神]]・[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] == 境内 == *本殿 *拝殿 *産土社 *顕彰殿 *祓所 *鐘楼 == 文化財 == ===重要文化財=== * 本殿 - [[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）建立。三間社流造、檜皮葺。 ===滋賀県選択無形民俗文化財=== * 松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。毎年1月第2土曜日に行われる。 == 所在地 == [[〒]]524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[琵琶湖線]] [[守山駅 (滋賀県)|守山駅]] 徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部氏|社かつへ]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]]

拝殿（手前）、本殿（奥） }} [[画像:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|顕彰殿]] '''勝部神社'''（かつべじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[守山市]]勝部にある[[神社]]。 正式名称は物部郷総社 勝部神社。戦前までは物部神社または物部大明神、勝部大明神と称されていた。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]で、旧物部郷の[[総社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）8月13日に物部宿禰広国（もののべのすくねひろくに）が物部郷勝部村の現在地に祖神を祀り、物部大明神と称したのが起源とされる。[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）正月には正六位上を、[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]）10月には従五位下の[[神位]]を授かる。 [[室町時代]]には[[近江国|近江]][[守護]][[六角氏]]により篤い信仰を受けており、六角軍が出陣する際にはその旗竿は決まって当社の竹林の竹を使用するほどであった。三間社流造檜皮葺の本殿は[[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼]]の寄進により再建されたものである。 [[元亀]]年間（[[1570年]] - [[1573年]]）には[[織田信長]]が[[野洲郡]]・[[栗太郡]]の起請文60通を当社に納めている。[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）8月には[[豊臣秀次]]によって神田の寄進と本殿の修理が行われている。 [[江戸時代]]には当地一帯に[[守山宿]]が設けられ、宿場町とともに隆盛した。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）に国の特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定されている。 江戸時代まで当社は物部神社または物部大明神と呼ばれてきたが、[[明治]]時代の初めに勝部神社に改めている。[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）、[[郷社]]に列せられる。 境内に[[鎮守の森]]を有している。 顕彰殿と忠魂碑は郷土出身の戦没者の霊を安めるための施設として戦前に建立されたものである。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 物部布津神（[[天火明命]]・[[ウマシマジ|宇麻志間知命]]・[[経津主神|布津主神]]） * 合祀神 - [[住吉三神|住吉神]]・[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] == 境内 == * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[明応]]6年（[[1497年]]）に[[六角高頼]]によって再建。三間社流造、檜皮葺。 * 拝殿 * 産土社 * 顕彰殿 * 祓所 * 鐘楼 - [[神仏習合]]時代の名残である。 * 社務所 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 本殿 === 滋賀県選択無形民俗文化財 === * 松明祭 - 通称「勝部の火祭り」。800年の歴史を持つ滋賀県の三大火祭りの一つ。毎年1月第2土曜日に行われる。保存団体は勝部神社松明組。 == 所在地 == 〒524-0041 滋賀県守山市勝部1丁目8-8 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[琵琶湖線]]）[[守山駅 (滋賀県)|守山駅]]から徒歩10分 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ 勝部神社（公式ホームページ）] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html 勝部神社（守山市）] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ 勝部神社（守山市観光物産協会）] * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/169/ 勝部神社（滋賀・びわ湖観光情報）] {{神道 横}}{{Commonscat-inline}}{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつへしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:物部氏|社かつへ]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings of the Muromachi period]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shrines related to the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] [[ja:勝部神社]] == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings of the Muromachi period]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shrines related to the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] [[ja:勝部神社]] {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Regional Sōja|ja|Category:総社 (地域)}} }} {{Sōja_shrines}} [[Category:Orrriginal_pages]] [[Category:Orrriginal_pages]] [[Category:勝部神社]] The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] [[ja:勝部神社]] == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) | established = {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649) | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube district, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In {{ill|Taika (era)|ja|大化}} 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Ninju era|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851), the shrine was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} in January. In {{ill|Gangyō|ja|元慶}} 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine received devout support from the {{ill|Rokkaku clan|ja|六角氏}}, the governors of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|元亀}} era (1570–1573), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} deposited 60 oaths from {{ill|Yasu District, Ōmi|ja|野洲郡}} and {{ill|Kurita District, Ōmi|ja|栗太郡}} at the shrine. In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 3 (1594), {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|ja|豊臣秀次}} donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area thrived as the site of the {{ill|Moriyama-juku|ja|守山宿|fr|6=en}} post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine includes a preserved {{ill|Chinju no Mori|ja|鎮守の森}} (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命}}, {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神}}) * '''Additional Deities''': {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|Meiō|ja|明応}} 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|ja|守山市}}, Katsube 1-chome 8-8, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}} == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|ja|守山駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}, operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Moriyama, Shiga]] (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.katsube-jinja.info/ Katsube Shrine (Official Website)] * [http://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/shokokanko/jinnjabukkaku_8.html Katsube Shrine (Moriyama City)] * [https://moriyamayamamori.jp/spot/spot-336/ Katsube Shrine (Moriyama Tourism)] *
