revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki {{Citation needed|date=December 27, 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |Shrine | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社) | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8 | position = {{coord|35|3|3|N|135|59|26.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsube Shrine}} | enshrined_deity = Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenokaguyama no Mikoto]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649) | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January) | main_hall = | image = [[File:Katsube-jinja02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (front), Honden (back) }} [[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]] '''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town. In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds. The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]) * '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] == Grounds == * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. * '''Haiden (Worship Hall)''' * '''Ubusuna Shrine''' * '''Kenjo Hall''' * '''Harai Place''' * '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period. * '''Shrine Office''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * '''Honden (Main Hall)''' === Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group. == Address == 〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] == Access == * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = deities of its local area
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
|Shrine
 | name = Katsube Shrine (勝部神社)
 | location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8

 | established = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649)
 | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
 | festivals = Matsuirushi Festival (2nd Saturday of January)

 }}

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]
'''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]].

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

== History ==

In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}.

In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden.

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town.

In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].

Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds.

The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]])
* '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]

== Grounds ==
* '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing.
* '''Haiden (Worship Hall)'''
* '''Ubusuna Shrine'''
* '''Kenjo Hall'''
* '''Harai Place'''
* '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period.
* '''Shrine Office'''

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Property ===
* '''Honden (Main Hall)'''

=== Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property ===
* '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group.

== Address ==
〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]

== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]

In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]
'''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]].

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

== History ==

In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}.

In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden.

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town.

In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].

Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds.

The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]])
* '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]

== Grounds ==
* '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|qq=|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing.
* '''Haiden (Worship Hall)'''
* '''Ubusuna Shrine'''
* '''Kenjo Hall'''
* '''Harai Place'''
* '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period.
* '''Shrine Office'''

== Cultural Properties ==

* '''Honden (Main Hall)'''

=== Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property ===
* '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group.

== Address ==

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]
'''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]].

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

== History ==

In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}.

In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden.

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town.

In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].

Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds.

The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]])
* '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]

== Grounds ==
* '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing.
* '''Haiden (Worship Hall)'''
* '''Ubusuna Shrine'''
* '''Kenjo Hall'''

* '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period.

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]
'''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}.

In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden.

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town.

In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].

Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

[[File:Katsube-jinja04s3872.jpg|thumb|200px|Kenjo Hall]]
'''Katsube Shrine''' (勝部神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube district, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. The shrine's official name is "Mononobe-gō Sōja Katsube Shrine" (物部郷総社 勝部神社). Before the war, it was called "Mononobe Shrine," "Mononobe Daimyōjin," or "Katsube Daimyōjin." It was the [[Sōja shrine]] (総社) of the former Mononobe district and held the Rank of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]].

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

== History ==

In [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 5 (649), on August 13, Mononobe no Sukune Hirokuni enshrined their ancestral deity at the current location in Katsube village of the Mononobe district, naming it "Mononobe Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Ninju|Ninju era]] 1 (851), the shrine was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} in January. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882), it received {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}.

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine received devout support from the [[:en:Rokkaku clan|Rokkaku clan]], the governors of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. When the Rokkaku army set out on campaigns, they always used bamboo from the shrine's bamboo grove for their flags. The three-bay nagare-zukuri style honden with cypress bark roofing was rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) through the contributions of {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}.

In the [[:en:Genki (era)|Tenshō]] era (1570–1573), [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] deposited 60 oaths from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District, Ōmi]] and [[:en:Kurita District, Shiga|Kurita District, Ōmi]] at the shrine. In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 3 (1594), [[:en:Toyotomi Hidetsugu|Toyotomi Hidetsugu]] donated rice fields and repaired the honden.

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the area thrived as the site of the [[:en:Moriyama-juku|Moriyama-juku]] post town.

In 1913, the honden was designated as a National Specially Protected Building, equivalent to the current designation of [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].

Until the Edo period, the shrine was called "Mononobe Shrine" or "Mononobe Daimyōjin," but it was renamed Katsube Shrine in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In 1931, it was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

The shrine includes a preserved [[:en:Chinju no Mori|Chinju no Mori]] (sacred forest) within its grounds.

The Kenjo Hall and Chūkonhi monument were constructed before World War II to honor local war dead.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* '''Primary deity''': Mononobe Futsukami ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amenohoakari]], {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志間知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]])
* '''Additional Deities''': [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]]

== Grounds ==
* '''Honden (Main Hall)''' ([[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt in [[:en:Meiō|Meiō]] 6 (1497) by {{ill|Rokkaku Takayori|ja|六角高頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkaku Takayori|1=Rokkaku Takayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2890348}}. Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing.
* '''Haiden (Worship Hall)'''
* '''Ubusuna Shrine'''
* '''Kenjo Hall'''
* '''Harai Place'''
* '''Bell Tower''' - A remnant of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period.
* '''Shrine Office'''

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Property ===
* '''Honden (Main Hall)'''

=== Shiga Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property ===
* '''Matsuirushi Festival''' - Commonly known as "Katsube Fire Festival." One of the three major fire festivals in Shiga Prefecture, it boasts a history of 800 years. Held annually on the second Saturday of January, it is preserved by the Katsube Shrine Matsuirushi Group.

== Address ==
〒524-0041 [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama, Shiga]], Katsube 1-chome 8-8, [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]

== Access ==
* 10-minute walk from [[:en:Moriyama Station|Moriyama Station (Shiga)]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]], operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
